question: I just created a user account in the system. What information will the user receive in their activation email?
agent_answer:
Based on the documentation, when you create a user account, the activation email will contain:

1. **Their login** (username) — used to log in to the system. The login is also displayed on the account activation page.
2. **An "Activate account" button** — clicking it takes the user to a page where they set their password (must be at least 9 characters long).

Once activated, the user can access the Cyber Protection service via the **Cyber Protect console** or the **Management Portal**.

**Source:** `01-activating-account.md` (Getting Started with Cyber Protection)

question: What is the minimum password length required when a user activates their account?
agent_answer:
The minimum password length is **9 characters**. When activating your account, you must ensure that your password is at least nine characters long.

**Source:** `01-activating-account.md` (Getting Started with Cyber Protection)

question: I received an activation email. How do I set my password and complete the account activation?
agent_answer:
## Account Activation

When you receive the activation email, follow these steps:

1. **Click the "Activate account" button** in the email message.
2. **Set your password** on the account activation page — it must be **at least 9 characters long**.

Your **login (username)** is also displayed both in the email and on the activation page for reference.

Once your password is set, you can log in to the **Cyber Protect console**:
1. Go to the Cyber Protection login page.
2. Enter your login → click **Next**.
3. Enter your new password → click **Next**.

> **Note:** If two-factor authentication (2FA) is enabled for your organization, you'll also need a one-time code from an authenticator app on your mobile device after entering your password.

**Sources:** `01-activating-account.md`, `05-accessing-cyber-protection-service.md`, `03-two-factor-authentication.md`

question: If two-factor authentication is enabled, will new users be prompted to set it up during account activation?
agent_answer:
Yes. According to the documentation, when 2FA is enabled for the organization, new users will be prompted to set up two-factor authentication during their account activation process.

From the **Two-factor authentication** doc:

> When 2FA is set up, you are required to enter your password (the first factor) and a one-time code (the second factor) to log in to the Cyber Protect console. The one-time code is generated by a special application that must be installed on your mobile phone or another device that belongs to you.

The activation flow works as follows:

1. The new user receives an activation email and clicks **Activate account** (source: `01-activating-account.md`)
2. They set their password (minimum 9 characters)
3. If 2FA is enabled for the organization, they will be required to configure the authenticator app and enroll their device as part of the activation/first-login process (source: `03-two-factor-authentication.md`)

This ensures that even new users cannot bypass 2FA — they must set it up before they can access the Cyber Protect console.
